svg.title: Commodity screening analysis summary
svg.desc: A structured diagram showing screening results for four selected commodities across five dimensions: drawdown ratio, gap ratio, noise ratio, full-period Sharpe, and trend fluency.

g.text: Shipping index (集运指数): best overall trend quality
Lowest noise (0.22), good fluency (1.01), solid 5Y Sharpe (1.05). Main risk: highest gap ratio (0.55) among the four — limit-up/down gap events are frequent.
Propylene (丙烯): best fluency, short track record
Highest trend fluency (1.21), low gap and noise ratios. But the contract is young (data from 2025), so 5Y history is absent — caution on regime stability.
Negative Sharpe every year since listing (worst fluency −0.87). Improving slowly but remains anti-trend. Not suitable for trend-following allocation.
Live hog (生猪): structurally low-noise but trend-resistant
Cleanest noise profile (0.27), low gap ratio (0.12). Yet Sharpe is negative and fluency is poor (−0.68). Cyclical hog price dynamics fight trend strategies.
2025–2026 turning point visible across all four
All selected commodities show improving yearly Sharpe into 2026. Propylene and shipping index turned strongly positive. Silicon and hog remain negative but are recovering — monitor for

svg.text: Recommended: Shipping index (if gap risk manageable), Propylene (watch for history accumulation)
Excluded: Industrial silicon and Live hog — both fail on trend fluency and multi-year Sharpe
